log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX-packed PE32 executable with tags indicating it's a spreader, has persistence mechanisms, and detects debug environments. I need to gather more IOC data to understand the full scope
The IOC summary shows no network activity (no IPs, domains, or HTTP), but there are 2 registry persistence keys, 4 dropped files, and 2 processes created. The highlighted API call 'IsDebuggerPresent'

I see 4 dropped files. Two have the same SHA256 (1955407b2fd523e3...) - CTS.exe and C:\Windows\CTS.exe - suggesting the main payload is CTS.exe. The other two files (iTQ7wCXM78ZHHqy.exe and 6cyOEWEvu1

The persistence mechanism is clear: it adds CTS.exe to the Windows Run key (HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run) with the value 'C' (likely a typo or shorthand for the full path, but th

The process activity shows the execution chain: the initial packed executable spawns C:\WINDOWS\CTS.exe, which then spawns iTQ7wCXM78ZHHqy.exe from Temp. The modules loaded include .NET runtime compon
